title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: That's all we're going to do today is custom bake.
Now I'm going to take these and put them in the living room on a table to cool.
I'm going to use the last bit of dough to make a hand print cookie.
That's just the butter that's so I'm going to put in two teaspoons of vanilla.
It's like a frosting assembly line here so I'm going to mix my frosting and get started.

title: will / shall
examples: And today we will bake them in the oven.
We've also got a lot of sprinkles, all different kinds of colors in shapes of sprinkles so we will also put those on the cookies to make them look really nice.

It took us two days to finish making all of these Christmas cookies but I think it will be worth it when we taste them. They smell delicious, they look delicious, I'm sure they must taste delicious as well. I'm trying an elephant shaped one because elephants are my favorite animal.

title: will be doing / will have done
examples: Even though making these cookies is a long process, I had a lot of fun doing it with my family and it's just a really great tradition that usually I have to miss out on when I'm in Korea so I was very excited to be home for the tradition this year. I hope you all have a very happy holiday season and I will be making a lot more videos like this while I'm in the U.S. so make sure to subscribe to my channel and check out the pinned comment below. I will see you all next time. Goodbye!

title: can / could / be able to
examples: So the frosting was homemade from scratch and now we are adding in a few drops of food coloring so we can have different colors of frosting to decorate the cookies.

title: have to / must
examples: You have to be a little bit careful because when it's raw dough there's a possibility of getting sick but it's a risk I'm willing to take.
It's so hard not to dig into these cookies right away but tomorrow is the day that we frost the cookies and decorate the cookies so I have to wait and be patient until then.

It took us two days to finish making all of these Christmas cookies but I think it will be worth it when we taste them. They smell delicious, they look delicious, I'm sure they must taste delicious as well. I'm trying an elephant shaped one because elephants are my favorite animal.

This brings me back to my childhood. I love these cookies and I've eaten them every Christmas season since I was very young. They're good on their own like this but I recommend dunking them in a glass of milk. That's a perfect combo. Cookies in milk and you must leave some out for Santa on Christmas night. I think Santa is going to be very happy at our house this year if we leave him some of these cookies. I have to show you my handprint cookie. It's always my favorite cookie to decorate because I like to paint my fingernails with frosting and add some jewelry like rings and bracelets and the best part about the handprint cookie is that it's the biggest one.

Even though making these cookies is a long process, I had a lot of fun doing it with my family and it's just a really great tradition that usually I have to miss out on when I'm in Korea so I was very excited to be home for the tradition this year. I hope you all have a very happy holiday season and I will be making a lot more videos like this while I'm in the U.S. so make sure to subscribe to my channel and check out the pinned comment below. I will see you all next time. Goodbye!
